channel.title: Point-Free Videos (Private feed for coen@tenthijeboonkkamp.nl)
channel.description: Point-Free is a video series that explores advanced topics in the Swift programming language. Each episode covers a topic that may seem complex and academic at first, but turns out to be quite simple. At the end of each episode we’ll ask “what’s the point?!”, so that we can bring the concepts back down to earth and show how these ideas can improve the quality of your code today. --- This is a private feed associated with the Point-Free account coen@tenthijeboonkkamp.nl. Please do not share this link with anyone else.
channel.itunes:author: Brandon Williams & Stephen Celis
channel.itunes:subtitle: Functional programming concepts explained simply.
channel.itunes:summary: Point-Free is a video series that explores advanced topics in the Swift programming language. Each episode covers a topic that may seem complex and academic at first, but turns out to be quite simple. At the end of each episode we’ll ask “what’s the point?!”, so that we can bring the concepts back down to earth and show how these ideas can improve the quality of your code today. --- This is a private feed associated with the Point-Free account coen@tenthijeboonkkamp.nl. Please do not share this link with anyone else.

item.title: Tour of Sharing: File Storage: Part 1
item.description: `@Shared` is far more than a glorified version of `@AppStorage`: it can be customized with additional persistence strategies, including the file storage strategy that comes with the library, which persists far more complex data than user defaults. We will create a complex, new feature that is powered by the file system.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Williams
Stephen Celis
item.itunes:author: Brandon Williams & Stephen Celis
item.itunes:subtitle: `@Shared` is far more than a glorified version of `@AppStorage`: it can be customized with additional persistence strategies, including the file storage strategy that comes with the library, which persists far more complex data than user defaults. We will create a complex, new feature that is powered by the file system.
item.itunes:summary: `@Shared` is far more than a glorified version of `@AppStorage`: it can be customized with additional persistence strategies, including the file storage strategy that comes with the library, which persists far more complex data than user defaults. We will create a complex, new feature that is powered by the file system.
item.itunes:title: Tour of Sharing: File Storage: Part 1

media:content.media:title: Tour of Sharing: File Storage: Part 1

item.title: Tour of Sharing: App Storage, Part 2
item.description: We show how the `@Shared` property wrapper, unlike `@AppStorage`, can be used _anywhere_, not just SwiftUI views. And we show how `@Shared` has some extra bells and whistles that make it easier to write maintainable Xcode previews and avoid potential bugs around "string-ly" typed keys and default values.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Williams
Stephen Celis
item.itunes:author: Brandon Williams & Stephen Celis
item.itunes:subtitle: We show how the `@Shared` property wrapper, unlike `@AppStorage`, can be used _anywhere_, not just SwiftUI views. And we show how `@Shared` has some extra bells and whistles that make it easier to write maintainable Xcode previews and avoid potential bugs around "string-ly" typed keys and default values.
item.itunes:summary: We show how the `@Shared` property wrapper, unlike `@AppStorage`, can be used _anywhere_, not just SwiftUI views. And we show how `@Shared` has some extra bells and whistles that make it easier to write maintainable Xcode previews and avoid potential bugs around "string-ly" typed keys and default values.
item.itunes:title: Tour of Sharing: App Storage, Part 2

media:content.media:title: Tour of Sharing: App Storage, Part 2
